title: Script R3: Quality Control Statistics
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bfptb","text":"This protocol outlines the quality control analysis measuring the percent relative abundance of 16S rRNA levels in the virome and whole metagenome, as well as the number of virome sequences mapping to the whole metagenome, and vice versa. These measures are used to validate the quality of the sequence datasets. Based on the methods from the following publication:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cs3dc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5kr3i","text":"Hannigan, Geoffrey D., et al. \"The Human Skin Double-Stranded DNA Virome: Topographical and Temporal Diversity, Genetic Enrichment, and Dynamic Associations with the Host Microbiome.\" mBio 6.5 (2015): e01578-15.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":184,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"blut6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b97em","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

locale: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7ktqm","text":"## [1] en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/C/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b8517","text":"## ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"be3ka","text":"## attached base packages: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dl16r","text":"## [1] stats graphics grDevices utils datasets methods base ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"92f4m","text":"## ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4p1ch","text":"## loaded via a namespace (and not attached): ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bdr6o","text":"## [1] magrittr_1.5    formatR_1.2    tools_3.2.0    htmltools_0.2.6 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"64int","text":"## [5] yaml_2.1.13    stringi_0.4-1    rmarkdown_0.7    knitr_1.10.5 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a7jrr","text":"## [9] stringr_1.0.0    digest_0.6.8    evaluate_0.7","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Load the proper R libraries. library(ggplot2)packageVersion( library(ggplot2) packageVersion("ggplot2") library(plyr) packageVersion("plyr") library(reshape2) packageVersion("reshape2") library(RColorBrewer) packageVersion("RColorBrewer") ## [1] '1.0.1' ## [1] '1.8.2' ## [1] '1.4.1' ## [1] '1.1.2'

First read in the expected values for the mock community sample. mock_community mock_community <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Mock_Community/mock_community_samples.txt") mock_community<-mock_community[,c("Phylum","Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus","Species", "Relative_Abundance")] mock_community_genus<-mock_community[,c("Genus", "Relative_Abundance")] mock_community_genus$Genus<-paste("p__", mock_community$Phylum, ";g__", mock_community$Genus, sep="") mock_community_genus<-aggregate(mock_community_genus$Relative_Abundance, list(mock_community_genus$Genus), FUN=sum) colnames(mock_community_genus)<-c("Genus", "MockCommunity")
Then read in the MetaPhlAn output for the mock community. WMS_all
Format the data for plotting, looking at the genus level. format_WMS_data_metaphlan

Convert to relative abundances. skinmet_data[,2] skinmet_data[,2]<-skinmet_data[,2]/sum(skinmet_data[,2]) return(skinmet_data) } WMS_updated_genus<-format_WMS_data_metaphlan(WMS_all) WMS_updated_genus$Taxon<-gsub(WMS_updated_genus$Taxon, pattern="Thermi", replacement="Deinococcus-Thermus") row.names(WMS_updated_genus)<-WMS_updated_genus$Taxon WMS_updated_genus$Taxon<-NULL row.names(WMS_updated_genus)<-gsub(row.names(WMS_updated_genus), pattern="k__Bacteria;p__", replacement="p__") row.names(WMS_updated_genus)<-gsub(row.names(WMS_updated_genus), pattern="c__.*;g__", replacement="g__") genus<-merge(mock_community_genus, WMS_updated_genus, by.x="Genus", by.y="row.names", all.x=TRUE) genus[is.na(genus)]<-0 genus_m<-melt(genus, id.vars=c("Genus")) colnames(genus_m)<-c("Genus","SampleID","RelativeAbundance") genus_m$Genus<-gsub(genus_m$Genus, pattern="^.*g__", replacement="", perl=TRUE) genus_m<-merge(unique(mock_community[,c("Phylum","Genus")]), genus_m, by="Genus") genus_m$Taxa<-paste("p__",genus_m$Phylum,";g__",genus_m$Genus, sep="")
Plot genus level comparisons. ggplot(genus_m[order(genus_m$Phylum,genus_m$Genus),], aes(x=SampleID, y=RelativeAbundance, fill=Taxa))+theme_bw()+geom_bar(stat =
Then, we calculated the 16S rRNA levels in each dataset. Import data frame. INPUT
Add column for level percent contamination. INPUT$Contamination

Generate bar graph with StdErr bars. INPUT_STATS INPUT_STATS <- ddply(INPUT, c("V3"), summarise, N = length(Contamination), mean = mean(Contamination), sd = sd(Contamination), se = sd / sqrt(N) ) T_TEST_P_VALUE <- t.test(INPUT$Contamination[INPUT$V3=="Virome_16S_hits"], INPUT$Contamination[INPUT$V3=="Whole_Metagenome_16S_hits"])$p.value ggplot(INPUT_STATS, aes(x=V3, y=mean)) + theme_bw() + geom_bar(fill="cornflowerblue", width=0.5, stat="identity") + geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=mean-se, ymax=mean+se), width=0.1) + geom_text(x=1, y=0.20, label=paste("p-value\n", T_TEST_P_VALUE)) + ggtitle("Percent Contamination Estimation By 16S") + ylab("Mean Relative Abundance of 16S rRNA") + xlab("Sample Type")

We next calculated the median numbers of virome sequences that map to the whole metagenome, and vice versa. Import data files and mapping file. VIR_TO_MET VIR_TO_MET <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Quality_Control_Stats/virome_to_metagenome_shared_results.tsv", sep="\t", header=FALSE) MET_TO_VIR <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Quality_Control_Stats/metagenome_to_virome_shared_results.tsv", sep="\t", header=FALSE) MAP <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Mapping_files/SkinMet_and_Virome_001_metadata.tsv", header=TRUE, sep="\t") MAP_CUT <- MAP[-which(MAP$NexteraXT_SampleID %in% c(NA)), ] MAP_CUT <- MAP_CUT[-which(MAP_CUT$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID %in% c(NA)), ] MET_TO_VIR_MERGE <- merge(MET_TO_VIR, MAP_CUT, by.x="V1", by.y="NexteraXT_SampleID") MET_TO_VIR_MERGE$Percent_match_met_to_vir <- 100 * MET_TO_VIR_MERGE$V3 / MET_TO_VIR_MERGE$V2 VIR_TO_MET_MERGE <- merge(VIR_TO_MET, MAP_CUT, by.x="V1", by.y="NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID") VIR_TO_MET_MERGE$Percent_match_vir_to_met <- 100 * VIR_TO_MET_MERGE$V3 / VIR_TO_MET_MERGE$V2

Get the range of percent matches. V_TO_M_RANGE V_TO_M_RANGE <- range(VIR_TO_MET_MERGE$Percent_match_vir_to_met) M_TO_V_RANGE <- range(MET_TO_VIR_MERGE$Percent_match_met_to_vir) MET_TO_VIR_MEAN <- mean(MET_TO_VIR_MERGE$Percent_match_met_to_vir) VIR_TO_MET_MEAN <- mean(VIR_TO_MET_MERGE$Percent_match_vir_to_met) MET_TO_VIR_SEM <- sd(MET_TO_VIR_MERGE$Percent_match_met_to_vir)/sqrt(length(MET_TO_VIR_MERGE$Percent_match_met_to_vir)) VIR_TO_MET_SEM <- sd(VIR_TO_MET_MERGE$Percent_match_vir_to_met)/sqrt(length(VIR_TO_MET_MERGE$Percent_match_vir_to_met)) MERGED_MEAN <- as.data.frame(c(MET_TO_VIR_MEAN,VIR_TO_MET_MEAN)) MERGED_SEM <- as.data.frame(c(MET_TO_VIR_SEM,VIR_TO_MET_SEM)) MERGED_NAMES <- as.data.frame(c("met_to_vir","vir_to_met")) MERGED <- cbind(MERGED_MEAN,MERGED_SEM,MERGED_NAMES) colnames(MERGED) <- c("mean","SEM","category")

Perform statistical analysis and plot. TTEST TTEST <- t.test(MET_TO_VIR_MERGE$Percent_match_met_to_vir, VIR_TO_MET_MERGE$Percent_match_vir_to_met) PVALUE <- TTEST$p.value ggplot(MERGED, aes(x=category, y=mean)) + theme_bw() + geom_bar(position=position_dodge(), stat="identity", fill="cornflowerblue", width=0.5) + geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=mean-SEM, ymax=mean+SEM), width=0.1, position=position_dodge(.9)) + geom_text(label=paste("p-value=",PVALUE,sep="\n"),y=16,x=1) + ggtitle("Compare Hits Between Communities") + xlab("Library Compared To Other Library") + ylab("Mean Percent Matching Reads")

Finally, we calculated the percentage of human contamination in the virome versus the whole metagenome samples. wm_total_seq_counts Calculates human contamination percent in WHOLE METAGENOME samples wm_total_seq_counts<-read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Whole_Microbiome_Sequence_Counts/trimmed_sequence_counts.tsv",header=FALSE) colnames(wm_total_seq_counts)<-c("SampleID","TotalTrimmedSeq") wm_clean_seq_counts<-read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Whole_Microbiome_Sequence_Counts/human_deconseq_sequence_counts.tsv",header=FALSE) colnames(wm_clean_seq_counts)<-c("SampleID","TotalCleanSeq") wm_counts<-merge(wm_total_seq_counts,wm_clean_seq_counts,by="SampleID") wm_counts$PercentContamination<-(wm_counts$TotalTrimmedSeq-wm_counts$TotalCleanSeq)/wm_counts$TotalTrimmedSeq * 100 wm_counts_sub<-wm_counts[,c("SampleID","PercentContamination")] wm_counts_sub$Type<-"WholeMetagenome" Calculates human contamination percent in WHOLE METAGENOME samples

Calculate human contamination in virome samples. v_total_seq_counts v_total_seq_counts<-read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Virome_Sequence_Counts/trimmed_sequence_counts.tsv",header=FALSE) colnames(v_total_seq_counts)<-c("SampleID","TotalTrimmedSeq") v_clean_seq_counts<-read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Virome_Sequence_Counts/human_deconseq_sequence_counts.tsv",header=FALSE) colnames(v_clean_seq_counts)<-c("SampleID","TotalCleanSeq") v_counts<-merge(v_total_seq_counts,v_clean_seq_counts,by="SampleID") v_counts$PercentContamination<-(v_counts$TotalTrimmedSeq-v_counts$TotalCleanSeq)/v_counts$TotalTrimmedSeq * 100

Remove row with total and plot graph. v_counts v_counts<-v_counts[-nrow(v_counts),] v_counts_sub<-v_counts[,c("SampleID","PercentContamination")] v_counts_sub$Type<-"Virome" counts_all<-rbind(wm_counts_sub, v_counts_sub) INPUT_STATS <- ddply(counts_all, c("Type"), summarise, N = length(PercentContamination), mean = mean(PercentContamination), sd = sd(PercentContamination), se = sd / sqrt(N) ) ggplot(INPUT_STATS, aes(x=Type, y=mean)) + theme_bw() + geom_bar(fill="cornflowerblue", width=0.5, stat="identity") + geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=mean-se, ymax=mean+se), width=0.1) + geom_text(x=1, y=5, label=paste("p-value\n", T_TEST_P_VALUE)) + ggtitle("Percent Human Contamination") + ylab("Mean Percentage of Human Contamination") + xlab("Sample Type")

Perform T test for p-value. T_TEST_P_VALUE T_TEST_P_VALUE <- t.test(counts_all$PercentContamination[counts_all$Type=="Virome"], counts_all$PercentContamination[counts_all$Type=="WholeMetagenome"])$p.value T_TEST_P_VALUE ## [1] 8.07085e-81
creator: Geoffrey D Hannigan
